examples.sentence: During the meeting, Sarah couldn't help but give John the eye when he mentioned the upcoming project deadline.
examples.translation: During the meeting, Sarah looked at John intently when he mentioned the upcoming project deadline.

examples.sentence: I noticed the waiter gave me the eye as if he was waiting for me to order.
examples.translation: I noticed the waiter looked at me with focused attention as if he was waiting for me to order.

examples.sentence: They were giving each other the eye across the crowded room at the party last night.

examples.sentence: When the teacher gave the students the eye, everyone immediately quieted down.
examples.translation: When the teacher looked at the students with focused attention, everyone immediately quieted down.

examples.sentence: After the fight broke out, the bouncer gave the troublemaker the heave-ho and escorted him out.
examples.translation: After the fight broke out, the bouncer forced the troublemaker to leave and escorted him out.

examples.sentence: If anyone causes problems during the event, the security team will give them the heave-ho immediately.
examples.translation: If anyone causes problems during the event, the security team will force them to leave immediately.

examples.sentence: The rowdy fans were given the heave-ho by the stadium staff after the game ended.

examples.sentence: When the company downsized, many employees were given the heave-ho without much notice.

examples.sentence: I heard that Jack got the heave-ho from his job last week due to budget cuts.
examples.translation: I heard that Jack was fired from his job last week due to budget cuts.

examples.sentence: The coach was given the heave-ho after the team’s poor performance this season.

examples.sentence: The referee gave the player the heave-ho after his second yellow card in the match.

examples.sentence: If a player gets too aggressive, the umpire won’t hesitate to give them the heave-ho.

examples.sentence: The security guard gave the unruly guest the heave-ho after he refused to calm down.
examples.translation: The security guard physically forced the unruly guest to leave after he refused to calm down.

examples.sentence: I watched as the bouncer gave the intoxicated man the heave-ho from the nightclub.
examples.translation: I watched as the bouncer physically pushed the intoxicated man out of the nightclub.

examples.sentence: If anyone blocks the entrance, the staff is instructed to give them the heave-ho immediately.
examples.translation: If anyone blocks the entrance, the staff is instructed to physically remove them immediately.

examples.sentence: The protesters were given the heave-ho by the police when they tried to enter the restricted area.
examples.translation: The protesters were physically forced out by the police when they tried to enter the restricted area.

examples.sentence: After the merger, several employees were given their cards as the new company restructured.
examples.translation: After the merger, several employees were dismissed as the new company restructured.

examples.sentence: I was given my cards last month after the department was downsized due to budget cuts.
examples.translation: I was dismissed last month after the department was downsized due to budget cuts.

examples.sentence: After the game, the dealer gave everyone their cards back before reshuffling.
examples.translation: After the game, the dealer handed everyone their physical cards back before reshuffling.

examples.sentence: Could you please give me my cards? I think you still have them from the last round.
examples.translation: Could you please hand me my physical cards? I think you still have them from the last round.

examples.sentence: The magician gave the audience their cards after the trick was over.
examples.translation: The magician returned the physical cards to the audience after the trick was over.

examples.sentence: She carefully gave me my cards so that none were bent or damaged.
examples.translation: She carefully handed me my physical cards so that none were bent or damaged.

examples.sentence: After years of loyal service, Tom was given the old heave-ho without any explanation.
examples.translation: After years of loyal service, Tom was dismissed abruptly without any explanation.

examples.sentence: I heard that the manager gave the old heave-ho to two employees last week due to poor performance.
examples.translation: I heard that the manager dismissed two employees abruptly last week due to poor performance.

examples.sentence: When the new CEO took over, several staff members were given the old heave-ho during the reorganization.
examples.translation: When the new CEO took over, several staff members were abruptly dismissed during the reorganization.

examples.sentence: The crowd got rowdy, and the security guards gave the old heave-ho to the troublemakers.
examples.translation: The crowd got rowdy, and the security guards physically pushed the troublemakers out.

examples.sentence: During the bar fight, the bouncer gave the old heave-ho to the aggressive patrons.
examples.translation: During the bar fight, the bouncer physically forced the aggressive patrons out.

examples.sentence: If anyone tries to cause a disturbance, they'll be given the old heave-ho by the staff.
examples.translation: If anyone tries to cause a disturbance, they'll be physically pushed out by the staff.

examples.sentence: The coach gave the old heave-ho to the player who repeatedly broke team rules.
examples.translation: The coach dismissed abruptly the player who repeatedly broke team rules.

examples.sentence: She was given the old heave-ho after the company decided to downsize the marketing team.
examples.translation: She was abruptly dismissed after the company decided to downsize the marketing team.

examples.sentence: Due to budget cuts, several employees were given the old heave-ho without prior warning.
examples.translation: Due to budget cuts, several employees were abruptly dismissed without prior warning.

examples.sentence: They gave him the old heave-ho just days before his contract was due to end.
examples.translation: They abruptly dismissed him just days before his contract was due to end.

examples.sentence: The customer service representative kept giving me the runaround whenever I asked about my refund.
examples.translation: The customer service representative kept delaying and frustrating me by giving vague answers whenever I asked about my refund.

examples.sentence: If the company continues to give us the runaround, we might have to take legal action.
examples.translation: If the company continues to delay and evade us, we might have to take legal action.

examples.sentence: He was given the runaround by multiple departments when trying to resolve the billing error.
examples.translation: He was delayed and frustrated by multiple departments when trying to resolve the billing error.

examples.sentence: The new employee was given the runaround, sent from HR to IT, then back to payroll without answers.
examples.translation: The new employee was caused to go from HR to IT, then back to payroll repeatedly without answers.

examples.sentence: When I called to fix my internet, the technician gave me the runaround by directing me to different support lines.
examples.translation: When I called to fix my internet, the technician caused me to go from one support line to another unnecessarily.

examples.sentence: The client complained that the sales team kept giving them the runaround instead of providing a clear quote.
examples.translation: The client complained that the sales team kept sending them from person to person instead of providing a clear quote.

examples.sentence: When I tried to get a straight answer, the manager gave me the runaround, dodging my questions skillfully.
examples.translation: When I tried to get a straight answer, the manager delayed and evaded by giving vague replies.

examples.sentence: They were given the runaround for weeks before the contractor finally showed up to repair the roof.
examples.translation: They were delayed and frustrated for weeks before the contractor finally showed up to repair the roof.

examples.sentence: Don't let them give you the runaround; insist on speaking to someone who can help you directly.
examples.translation: Don't let them delay or evade you; insist on speaking to someone who can help you directly.

examples.sentence: The student was given the runaround by the administration when trying to change his course schedule.
examples.translation: The student was frustrated and delayed by the administration when trying to change his course schedule.

examples.sentence: The thief tried to grab her purse, but she managed to give him the slip in the crowded market.
examples.translation: The thief tried to grab her purse, but she physically freed herself and escaped stealthily in the crowded market.

examples.sentence: I was being followed for blocks, but I finally gave the detective the slip by ducking into a café.
examples.translation: I was being followed for blocks, but I physically escaped the detective by quickly entering a café.

examples.sentence: They gave the security guards the slip by sneaking out the back door during the commotion.
examples.translation: They physically escaped the security guards by sneaking out the back door during the commotion.

examples.sentence: When the paparazzi got too close, the celebrity gave them the slip and disappeared into a side alley.
examples.translation: When the paparazzi got too close, the celebrity evaded them by quickly escaping into a side alley.

examples.sentence: The suspect gave the police the slip during the chase by hiding in an abandoned building.
examples.translation: The suspect evaded the police during the chase by hiding in an abandoned building.

examples.sentence: She managed to give her ex the slip after the awkward encounter at the party.
examples.translation: She managed to avoid her ex by quickly leaving after the awkward encounter at the party.

examples.sentence: He quickly gave his pursuers the slip by slipping into the crowded subway station unnoticed.
examples.translation: He physically freed himself from his pursuers by moving stealthily into the crowded subway station.

examples.sentence: The cat gave the dog the slip by darting under the fence before it could be caught.
examples.translation: The cat physically escaped the dog by quickly moving under the fence.

examples.sentence: I almost got caught, but I gave the security guard the slip by blending into the crowd.
examples.translation: I physically freed myself from the security guard by moving stealthily into the crowd.

examples.sentence: They gave the police the slip by taking a different route through the park.
examples.translation: They physically escaped the police by moving stealthily through the park.

examples.sentence: Please give me time to finish this report before you review it.
examples.translation: Please allow me sufficient time to finish this report before you review it.

examples.sentence: The teacher gave the students time to complete the exam within the allotted period.
examples.translation: The teacher granted the students sufficient time to complete the exam within the allotted period.

examples.sentence: If you give her time, she will be able to learn the new software comfortably.
examples.translation: If you allow her enough time, she will be able to learn the new software comfortably.

examples.sentence: We were given time by the organizers to prepare our presentations thoroughly.
examples.translation: We were granted sufficient time by the organizers to prepare our presentations thoroughly.

examples.sentence: After the argument, he asked for some space and time to think things over.
examples.translation: After the argument, he asked to be allowed an interval to reconsider his feelings.

examples.sentence: Give her time to adjust to the new environment before expecting her to perform at her best.
examples.translation: Allow her an interval to adapt to the new environment before expecting her to perform at her best.

examples.sentence: If you give me some time, I might change my mind about the proposal.
examples.translation: If you allow me an interval, I might reconsider my opinion about the proposal.

examples.sentence: The professor gave the students to understand the complex theory through detailed examples.
examples.translation: The professor caused the students to comprehend the complex theory through detailed examples.

examples.sentence: I gave him to understand that the deadline was non-negotiable during our meeting.
examples.translation: I caused him to comprehend that the deadline was non-negotiable during our meeting.

examples.sentence: She gave me to understand that the project would require additional resources soon.
examples.translation: She caused me to comprehend that the project would require additional resources soon.

examples.sentence: By mentioning the budget cuts, he gave me to understand that layoffs might be coming.
examples.translation: By mentioning the budget cuts, he implied indirectly that layoffs might be coming.

examples.sentence: The tone of his message gave her to understand that he was unhappy with the decision.
examples.translation: The tone of his message indirectly indicated to her that he was unhappy with the decision.

examples.sentence: They gave us to understand that the negotiations were not going well without saying it outright.
examples.translation: They indirectly implied to us that the negotiations were not going well without stating it directly.

examples.sentence: The teacher gave the class to understand the importance of punctuality through a heartfelt story.
examples.translation: The teacher caused the class to comprehend the importance of punctuality through a heartfelt story.

examples.sentence: He gave me to understand the procedure by walking me through each step carefully.
examples.translation: He caused me to comprehend the procedure by walking me through each step carefully.

examples.sentence: Our guide gave us to understand the history of the monument during the tour.
examples.translation: Our guide caused us to comprehend the history of the monument during the tour.

examples.sentence: They gave the team to understand the new policy changes during the morning briefing.
examples.translation: They caused the team to comprehend the new policy changes during the morning briefing.

examples.sentence: When Tim broke the vase, his mother gave him what for in no uncertain terms.
examples.translation: When Tim broke the vase, his mother severely reprimanded him.

examples.sentence: If you keep arriving late, the boss will definitely give you what for.
examples.translation: If you keep arriving late, the boss will certainly punish or reprimand you.

examples.sentence: After the argument, she gave him what for about his careless remarks.
examples.translation: After the argument, she severely rebuked him for his careless remarks.

examples.sentence: The teacher gave the student what for after catching him cheating on the exam.
examples.translation: The teacher severely reprimanded the student after catching him cheating on the exam.

examples.sentence: He was given what for by the coach for missing practice again.
examples.translation: He was severely rebuked by the coach for missing practice again.

examples.sentence: If you break the rules, you will be given what for by the authorities.
examples.translation: If you break the rules, you will be punished or reprimanded by the authorities.

examples.sentence: She gave her brother what for after he borrowed her car without permission.
examples.translation: She severely scolded her brother after he borrowed her car without permission.

examples.sentence: The principal gave the student what-for for vandalizing school property.
examples.translation: The principal angrily reprimanded the student for vandalizing school property.

examples.sentence: You’ll get what-for if you keep ignoring the safety regulations.
examples.translation: You will be berated or spoken to angrily if you keep ignoring the safety regulations.

examples.sentence: Mom gave me what-for after she found out I lied about finishing my homework.
examples.translation: Mom angrily scolded me after she found out I lied about finishing my homework.

examples.sentence: He gave his son what-for for breaking the neighbor’s window accidentally.
examples.translation: He angrily spoke to his son in rebuke for breaking the neighbor’s window accidentally.

examples.sentence: The coach gave the player what-for after a poor performance during the match.
examples.translation: The coach angrily admonished the player after a poor performance during the match.

examples.sentence: She gave her colleague what-for over the missed deadline in a firm tone.
examples.translation: She spoke angrily to her colleague in rebuke over the missed deadline.

examples.sentence: They gave the employee what-for for violating company policies repeatedly.
examples.translation: They spoke angrily to the employee in rebuke for repeatedly violating company policies.

examples.sentence: Could you please give me some wood for the campfire before it gets dark?

examples.sentence: The carpenter gave the apprentice wood to practice carving on during the workshop.
examples.translation: The carpenter handed the apprentice pieces of wood to practice carving on during the workshop.

examples.sentence: They gave us wood to build the garden fence last weekend.

examples.sentence: She gave her son some wood blocks to play with while she prepared dinner.
examples.translation: She handed her son some wooden blocks to play with while she prepared dinner.

examples.sentence: The romantic movie definitely gave him wood, judging by his flushed face afterwards.
examples.translation: The romantic movie caused him to have an erection, judging by his flushed face afterwards.

examples.sentence: She knew her charm would give him wood, and she smiled knowingly.
examples.translation: She knew her charm would sexually arouse him to the point of erection, and she smiled knowingly.

examples.sentence: Whenever he sees her, it seems to give him wood no matter where they are.
examples.translation: Whenever he sees her, it seems to sexually arouse him to the point of erection no matter where they are.

examples.sentence: I've never played tennis before, but I'll give it a go this weekend with some friends.
examples.translation: I have never played tennis before, but I will attempt it this weekend with some friends.

examples.sentence: Why don't you give the new recipe a go and see how it turns out?

examples.sentence: If you’re unsure about the job, just give it a go and learn as you progress.

examples.sentence: I decided to give the old bicycle a go after fixing the brakes.

examples.sentence: She gave the new software a go to see if it would improve her workflow.
examples.translation: She tried using the new software to see if it would improve her workflow.

examples.sentence: We gave the hiking trail a go, even though none of us had done it before.
examples.translation: We attempted the hiking trail even though none of us had done it before.

examples.sentence: After some hesitation, Tom gave the guitar a go and managed to play a few chords.
examples.translation: After some hesitation, Tom tried playing the guitar and managed to play a few chords.

examples.sentence: I think I'll give the party a miss this weekend and just relax at home instead.
examples.translation: I think I'll deliberately avoid the party this weekend and just relax at home instead.

examples.sentence: She gave the meeting a miss because she was feeling under the weather.
examples.translation: She deliberately skipped the meeting because she was feeling unwell.

examples.sentence: We decided to give the concert a miss since the tickets were too expensive.
examples.translation: We decided to deliberately avoid the concert since the tickets were too expensive.
